facts.text: Hastings was founded in the 1890s as a railroad‑oriented farming community built to supply potatoes to Henry Flagler’s resort hotels in St. Augustine.
The town earned the nickname “Potato Capital of Florida” after cultivating 21,000 acres of potatoes, while also growing cabbage, onions, eggplant, and ornamental plants.
Hastings dissolved its incorporated status in 2018 after an 82% majority vote, and Johns County officially took control on March 1, 2018.
Today, Hastings hosts the Field to Fork food exhibit, celebrating its agricultural roots and continuing to showcase local potato and vegetable production.
